mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: ZIP Code for Large Samples: Use a Z-test, Assume Independence, know the Parameter (mean or proportion).

mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 2
mnemonics.content: Large samples are NICE: Normality (assumed by CLT), Independence, Comparison, Estimation.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: The Central Limit Theorem (CLT) allows us to use the normal distribution (z-scores) for the sampling distribution of means or proportions, even if the underlying population isn't normal, provided the sample size is large enough (n > 30 is a common rule of thumb).

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The standard error for the difference between two independent sample means is calculated as: SE = sqrt[(s1²/n1) + (s2²/n2)], which combines the variability of both groups.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: The null hypothesis (H0) for a comparison test is typically that there is no difference between the population parameters (e.g., μ1 - μ2 = 0 or p1 - p2 = 0). The test determines if the observed difference is statistically significant.
